name: Mushroom Croustades
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the tops off the rolls and set aside as lids.
2. Scoop out most of the crumb from the rolls, being careful not to pierce the crusts.
3. Crush garlic with a pinch of salt (about 1/2 teaspoon), fresh chopped rosemary, and black pepper in butter. Mash together thoroughly.
4. Spread the mixture evenly over the inside of the hollowed-out rolls.
5. Season cream generously with salt and pepper, add fresh chopped rosemary (1/2 teaspoon) and let it infuse.
6. Slice mushrooms thickly.
7. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
8. Place rolls on a baking tray and set lids beside them.
9. Bake for 10 minutes until heated through and lightly crisp.
10. In a large frying pan, melt 1/2 oz butter.
11. Add mushrooms and cook, stirring frequently, for 4-5 minutes to reduce and intensify flavor.
12. Pour in cream and let it bubble gently.
13. Turn mushrooms and stir until evenly coated with the sauce.
14. Adjust seasoning away from heat.
15. Fill croustades with creamy mushroom mixture, place lids on top, and serve immediately with a salad.
